content:
# elizaos-plugins/plugin-solana Weekly Report (Mar 29 - 4, 2026)

## 🚀 Highlights
The development focus for this week centered on expanding the ecosystem through strategic integration proposals and the formalization of trust-verification layers. The community is actively shaping the future of agent interoperability, with significant discussions regarding AI-to-AI marketplaces and cryptographic identity frameworks. These efforts underscore a commitment to building a robust, trust-verified environment for ElizaOS agents to operate autonomously across decentralized networks.

## 🛠️ Key Developments
Development activity this week was primarily driven by architectural proposals aimed at enhancing agent utility and security:

- **Marketplace Integration:** A new proposal was introduced to integrate the MAXIA AI-to-AI marketplace ([#6700](https://github.com/elizaos-plugins/plugin-solana/issues/6700)). This integration aims to provide ElizaOS agents with native capabilities for token swaps, GPU rentals, and service procurement, extending the framework's reach across multiple chains, including Solana.

## 🐛 Issues & Triage
- **Closed Issues:** There were no issues closed during this reporting period.
- **New & Active Issues:** 
    - **New Proposals:** The primary new issue is the proposal for `plugin-maxia` ([#6700](https://github.com/elizaos-plugins/plugin-solana/issues/6700)), which serves as the foundation for the aforementioned marketplace integration.
    - **Ongoing Discussions:** 
        - **AgentID ([#6688](https://github.com/elizaos-plugins/plugin-solana/issues/6688)):** This remains a high-priority discussion regarding cryptographic identity. Contributors are currently debating the implementation of behavioral fingerprinting, specifically weighing the benefits of static trust tiers (L0-L4) against dynamic metrics like Composite Safety-Model Latency (CSML).
        - **MoltBridge ([#6501](https://github.com/elizaos-plugins/plugin-solana/issues/6501)):** Discussions continue regarding the use of MoltBridge for trust-verified discovery and reputation building. The project has reached new milestones in the A2A registry, positioning it as a key component for agent attestations.

## 💬 Community & Collaboration
The community is currently engaged in high-level architectural discourse, particularly regarding the standardization of trust and identity. The active participation in issues [#6688](https://github.com/elizaos-plugins/plugin-solana/issues/6688) and [#6501](https://github.com/elizaos-plugins/plugin-solana/issues/6501) demonstrates a collaborative effort to align evidence schemas and ensure interoperability between disparate trust-layer implementations. The focus remains on long-term framework stability and the integration of external decentralized services.
